_meta.name: wpr-finance-tools local constants
_meta.update_process: market.* from wpr-property-transactions ledger; property_tax.* verified against Marathon County Treasurer mill-rate publication each December; insurance/loan defaults reviewed manually
_meta.contract_rule: Widgets import this file at build time. Every consuming widget MUST validate required keys at module load and throw on any missing key. No runtime fetch, no fallback values.

median_sale_price.note: Median of 236 single-family sales, including 18 vacant-lot sales (median $52,000) — built homes alone ran $275,000.
median_sale_price.source: wpr-property-transactions ledger, data/transactions.json generated 2026-07-02 (DOR RETR; editorial policy applied in-pipeline: genuine sales only, >= $1,000; filtered to county = Marathon, property_use = Single family)

median_trend.note: Monthly median of Marathon County single-family sales (the transactions widget's Residential group). Complete months only — the in-progress month is excluded until it closes.

property_tax.note: Effective full-value rates: (total property tax - school levy credit) / DOR equalized value. Source: WI DOR 'Town, Village and City Taxes 2025' (https://www.revenue.wi.gov/SLFReportscotvc/tvc25.PDF), column 8, taxes levied 2025 / collected 2026. Excludes lottery and first-dollar credits, so slightly high for owner-occupants. All 61 Marathon County tax districts listed; municipalities straddling a county line are rated on their Marathon County portion and labeled as such. First entry is the calculator's default selection. Every rate machine-verified against the report's raw tax/credit/value columns; sums reconcile to the printed county totals to the dollar. lottery_credit (primary residences) and first_dollar_credit (any improved parcel) are 2025-26 dollar amounts from DOR's credit calculation notices (credit = school tax rate x $27,000 / $9,000 max credit values); where a municipality spans multiple school districts (school_districts > 1) the value is the mean of the district credits (worst intra-county spread $78.56/yr). Every credit machine-verified against rate x max value.

insurance.note: Wisconsin average annual premium by amount of insurance, all owner-occupied policy forms (Total column). Dwelling coverage amount is approximated by sale price in the calculator. 2022 is the latest state-level data insurers report to regulators; premiums have risen since — treat as a floor, and the footer discloses the vintage.
insurance.source: NAIC Dwelling Fire, Homeowners Owner-Occupied, and Homeowners Tenant and Condominium/Cooperative Unit Owner's Insurance Report: Data for 2022 (published May 2025), Table 4, Wisconsin (https://content.naic.org/sites/default/files/publication-hmr-zu-homeowners-report.pdf)

pmi.note: Applied when down payment < 20%. Industry-typical midpoint; not lender-specific.

closing_costs.note: Buyer-side estimate (lender, title, escrow, recording). WI real estate transfer fee ($3/$1,000) is seller-paid and excluded.

affordability.note: Max share of gross income spent on housing (PITI + PMI) — the standard 28% front-end ratio. Industry convention, not lender-specific; drives the 'income to afford this' line.

sponsor.note: All null until a sponsor signs — the widget then shows the dashed placeholder pitch. When set: name (and optional logo, as a data: URI or bundled asset path) replaces the placeholder, and the 'Talk to a local lender' tag links to url. name and url must be set together. The 'presented by' disclosure tag always renders — editorial-integrity requirement, not decoration.

loan_defaults.note: UI starting values only — user-adjustable, so weekly rate movement doesn't require a deploy.
